title: psychotria sarmentosa var. membranacea
intro: Psychotria sarmentosa var. membranacea, also known as Gotu Kola, is a type of plant that belongs to the Rubiaceae family. It is a perennial herb native to the tropical regions of Asia and Africa, and is traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties. Gotu Kola is valued for its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its ability to promote wound healing, improve cognitive function, and boost the immune system. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, capsules, and extracts, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including anxiety, depression, and skin conditions such as eczema and psoriasis.
